ab: I sikano ko loma’ niyam, i kaemang ho, namitilid to, palinah tayra i takaw, itira sato ko ’orip i tokay.
ch: 小時候我們家住在鹿野，上學以後，就搬到了高雄，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in sikano. After I started school, we moved to Kaohsiung and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: Na mitilid to iTasiye, patodong sanay kako a minanam to pakayniay i serangawan no Yin-co-min, cipinangay to a mafana’ to makahirahiraay a serangawn no Yin-co-min-co i Taywan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: Saka, anini mihecaan o pahanhanan to no kafaedetan i, minokay tayra i niyaro’ miliso’ to malitengay kako ato kaka to fa’inayan, hay mafana’ to niyah a finacadana ato serangawan.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: Falo polo’ to ko mihecaan noya malitengay, kawrira, nengneng han mato tahatenokay ho ko mihecaan nira.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來他的年齡好像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: Licay han no mako cingra ono icowacowaay ko kafana’an iso a sowal, calicpic ko sowal nira to no ’Amis, mafana’ haca to sowal no Ponong, Ngayngay ato no Dipong a sowal.
ch: 我問他你會哪些語言，他的阿美語說得很流利，也會說布農語、客家語和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in ’Amis, as well as Bunun, Hakka and Japanese.

ab: Nalicayen fana’ sato kako, hanaw o nano kaemangan ho cingra minanamay to sowal no Dipong, o Ponong ko fafahi, i katayalan nira adihay ko kananaman a Ngayngay, mansa kahirahira ko kafana’an nira to sowal.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是布農族人，而他工作上認識許多客家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Bunun, and he knows many Hakkas at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: Ya milicay kami i, sowal sa to maamaan to pakayniay mi’adopay a pinangan no litengan ato taneng.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: Ano awa awa ko kafana’an niyam mitengil i, kawrira, o tingwa ko sapiluing niyam, ta nga’ay kami miliyaw a mitengil.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: Awaay ko kafanafana’an no mako to niyah a serangawn ato finacadan, pakayni takowanan mangodo kako a miharateng.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己的族群和文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: Matongalmatongal to ko fana’ i, na o ’Aims ko tireng, o tadamasaromaay to, o kanono’an to a malita’ang.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為阿美族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a ’Aims is unique and something to be proud of.
